Details:
Ultaviolet irradiation at a wavelength of 2O8-2 ions, and photosynthesizers such as metheylene blue and rose bengal. Solar irradiation also results in carbofuran degradation in the presence of catalysts such as titanium dioxide.
The degradation of carbofuran by UV irradiation is influenced by pH, temperature, and the carbofuran concentration. With no catalyst present, UV irradiation is not influenced by pH.  In the presence of a catalyst, however, increases in pH can result in increased degradation of carbofuran.  Similarly, higher water temperatures generally resulted in increases carbofuran degradation. 
One study showed that the degradation of carbofuran using UV irradiation was faster in natural waters (seawater and pond water) than in distilled water, demonstrating that increased presence of dissolved salts and other compounds increase degradation rates of carbofuran.
